commands_executed: echo '05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost' >> /etc/crontab

execution_details.stdout:
SHELL=/bin/bash
PATH=/sbin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin
MAILTO=root

# For details see man 4 crontabs

# Example of job definition:
# .---------------- minute (0 - 59)
# |  .------------- hour (0 - 23)
# |  |  .---------- day of month (1 - 31)
# |  |  |  .------- month (1 - 12) OR jan,feb,mar,apr ...
# |  |  |  |  .---- day of week (0 - 6) (Sunday=0 or 7) OR sun,mon,tue,wed,thu,fri,sat
# |  |  |  |  |
# *  *  *  *  * user-name  command to be executed

execution_details.command: echo '05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost' >> /etc/crontab

scan_output: Vulnerability aide_scan_notification not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message:
The AIDE package was found to be already installed. I have updated `/etc/crontab` to include a scheduled AIDE integrity check that sends an email notification to `root@localhost`.

**Changes applied:**
- Appended the following cron job to `/etc/crontab`:
  `05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost`

per_turn.command: echo '05 4 * * * root /usr/sbin/aide --check | /bin/mail -s "$(hostname) - AIDE Integrity Check" root@localhost' >> /etc/crontab
